# Argustack

> RAG engine for project intelligence — pull Jira, Git, GitHub into local PostgreSQL, then ask AI about your project. Includes JetBrains IDE plugin with kanban board where columns are Claude Code skills.

## What it does

CLI tool that downloads your project data into a local database, then gives Claude / any LLM direct access to query it.

Five sources of truth:
- **Jira** — issues, comments, changelogs, worklogs, links (what was planned)
- **Jira CSV** — import from CSV export for teams without Jira API access
- **Git** — commits, per-file diffs, issue cross-references (what was coded)
- **GitHub** — PRs, reviews, comments, releases (what was reviewed)
- **Database** — coming soon (what actually exists in production)

Each source is independent — own adapter, own use case, own sync command.

All data stays on localhost in PostgreSQL (Docker). Nothing leaves your machine.

## How users interact

Two modes — interactive (human) and non-interactive (AI agents, CI/CD):

```bash
npm i -g argustack

# Interactive — prompts guide you through setup
argustack init

# Non-interactive — all values via CLI flags, no prompts
argustack init --no-interactive \
  --dir ~/project --source jira,git,github \
  --jira-url "https://team.atlassian.net" \
  --jira-email "user@co.com" --jira-token "ATATT3x..." \
  --jira-projects PAP,MKT \
  --git-repo /path/to/repo \
  --github-token "github_pat_..." --github-owner org --github-repo repo

# Then:
docker compose up -d       # start database
argustack sync             # pull all data from configured sources
argustack sync jira        # pull specific source
argustack sync github      # pull GitHub PRs/reviews/releases separately
argustack sync csv         # import from Jira CSV export
argustack push             # push local board tasks to Jira
argustack embed            # generate OpenAI embeddings for semantic search
argustack mcp install      # connect to Claude Desktop as MCP server
```

Non-interactive flags: `--no-interactive`, `--dir`, `--source`, `--jira-url`, `--jira-email`, `--jira-token`, `--jira-projects`, `--git-repo`, `--github-token`, `--github-owner`, `--github-repo`, `--csv-file`, `--db-port`, `--pgweb-port`.

### Estimate Tool — Data Source Awareness

The estimate tool adapts its output based on available data:
- **With Jira API + Git**: actual work hours (time_spent, worklogs, commit spans) → per-developer predictions in hours with "without bugs" / "with bugs" breakdown
- **With CSV only**: no effort data → shows **Resolution Timeline** in business days (weekdays only) with clear warning that this is lead time (backlog wait + development + review), not active development time

After sync, users open Claude Desktop or Claude Code and ask questions in natural language.
Claude queries the local database via 15 MCP tools and answers with full project context.

AI agents can run `argustack init --no-interactive` autonomously — no terminal interaction needed.

## MCP Tools (15 total)

**Jira:** query_issues, get_issue, issue_stats, pull_jira, list_projects
**Git:** query_commits, issue_commits, commit_stats
**GitHub:** query_prs, issue_prs, query_releases
**Cross-source:** issue_timeline (full cross-source timeline: changelogs + commits + PRs for one issue), semantic_search (pgvector embeddings)
**Prediction:** estimate (predict task duration for a specific developer — with effort data: two predictions "without bugs" and "with bugs" based on similar tasks, personal coefficient, and component familiarity; with CSV only: resolution timeline in business days showing lead time from creation to close)
**System:** workspace_info

## IDE Plugin (plugins/jetbrains/)

Kanban board for JetBrains IDEs. Cards are Markdown files, columns are Claude Code skills from `.claude/skills/`. Drag a card to a skill column to execute it.

- Kotlin 2.3 + JCEF React webview
- Cards live in `Docs/Tasks/{Backlog,InProgress,Done}/{epic}/`
- Workflows group skills into named pipelines
- Done column has configurable time filter
- Auto-generates `Docs/Tasks/README.md` on first load
- State in `.kanban.json`, file system is source of truth

Install: download ZIP from GitHub Releases, Settings > Plugins > Install from Disk.

## Architecture — Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters)

Driving adapters (entries): cli/, mcp/
Driven adapters (external systems): adapters/jira/, adapters/git/, adapters/github/, adapters/postgres/
Dependency Rule: cli/,mcp/ → use-cases/ → core/ports ← adapters/

```
src/core/types/    — Domain types (Issue, PullRequest, Commit, Config) — zero dependencies
src/core/ports/    — Interfaces (ISourceProvider, IGitProvider, IGitHubProvider, IStorage)
src/adapters/      — Driven adapters (jira/, git/, github/, csv/, postgres/, openai/)
src/use-cases/     — Business logic (PullUseCase, PullGitUseCase, PullGitHubUseCase, EmbedUseCase)
src/cli/           — Driving adapter — CLI entry point, commands
src/mcp/           — Driving adapter — MCP server for Claude Desktop / Claude Code (15 tools)
```

## Database tables

**Jira:** issues (including original_estimate, time_spent, assignee_id, reporter_id), issue_comments, issue_changelogs, issue_worklogs, issue_links
**Git:** commits, commit_files, commit_issue_refs
**GitHub:** pull_requests, pr_reviews, pr_comments, pr_files, pr_issue_refs, releases

Cross-references: commit messages and PR titles mentioning `PROJ-123` are automatically linked to Jira issues.

## Key design decisions

- Download ALL fields from Jira (`fields=*all`) — every custom field preserved
- Store raw JSON as-is in `raw_json` JSONB column — nothing filtered
- AsyncGenerator for streaming large datasets (100k+ issues)
- Multi-repo Git support — `GIT_REPO_PATHS` accepts comma-separated paths, one GitProvider per repo, shared storage
- Progress indicators during sync — ports expose optional count methods (`getIssueCount()`, `getCommitCount()`, `getPrCount()`) for displaying `150/506 issues (30%)`
- Git MCP tools (`query_commits`, `issue_commits`, `commit_stats`) accept optional `repo_path` filter for multi-repo workspaces
- Each workspace is isolated — own Docker, own credentials, own database
- Jira CSV import — universal adapter for any Jira instance, dynamic header detection, supports worklogs/comments/links/custom fields
- ESM modules, TypeScript strict mode, Vitest for testing
